result:
I’d make every human mind default to curiosity instead of fear when it meets something unfamiliar.  
Not naïve trust, just the reflex that says “tell me more” before it says “get away.”

One small shift in neural grammar—curiosity first, defense second—would rewrite politics, markets, borders, religions, dinner tables, and every algorithm we ever build. Overnight, “different” would stop being a threat tag and start being a question mark; the energy we now pour into walls, bribes, and arsenies would reroute into experiments, art, and antibiotics. We’d still disagree, but we’d argue over facts we both want to know rather than myths we both want to protect.

It’s the cheapest, fastest world-changing hack I know: no new gadget, no utopian charter, just a toggle in the running order of emotions. Curiosity is lighter than fear; once it’s in the lead, everything else gets easier to lift.

prompt: If you could change the world in one way, what would it be?
